# Interview Guide

Save under `docs/elicitation/guides/interview-<role>.md`.

## Metadata

- Audience role:
- Duration target:
- Lead BA:
- Status: draft | review | agreed

## Objective

What do we need to learn from this interview that we cannot learn elsewhere?

## Pre-Read For Interviewee

- Item.

## Opening (5 min)

Set context, get consent to record/take notes, explain how the output will
be used.

## Discovery Section (open-ended)

Use these to learn the world before testing hypotheses.

- Walk me through how you do <process> today.
- What part of <process> takes the most time?
- Where do mistakes most often happen?
- What does a great outcome look like for you?
- What do you do when <edge case> happens?

## Deep-Dive Section (semi-structured)

Topic-specific questions; expect to skip ones the interviewee already
answered.

- Topic A
  - Question A1.
  - Question A2.
- Topic B
  - Question B1.

## Closing

- Is there anything important I did not ask?
- Who else should I talk to about this?
- What documents or screens would help me understand this better?

## Anti-Patterns (do not do)

- Leading questions ("Don't you think it would be better if ...?").
- Solution-disguised-as-question ("Would a button here help?").
- Asking the interviewee to design the system.
- Asking yes/no questions when you need narrative.

## Post-Session Checklist

- [ ] Session log started.
- [ ] Raw statements extracted.
- [ ] Follow-up questions captured.
- [ ] Glossary candidates flagged.
- [ ] Traceability rows added for new raw statements.

## History

- YYYY-MM-DD: created.
